name: Okonomiyaki
recipeIngredient: 4 Whole scallions, cut into half-lengthwise and 1-inch strips
1/2 cup Cooked shrimp, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Toast the nori, crumble, and set aside.
2. Combine all sauce ingredients in a small saucepan and simmer for 30 seconds, stirring constantly. Remove from the heat and cool to room temperature.
3. Beat the eggs in a large bowl. Add the flour and water and continue beating until you have a batter the consistency of pancake batter. Add the sake and salt. Fold in the cabbage, carrots, and scallions. Mix the batter together evenly.
4. Each okonomiyaki will use 1/4 cup of batter. Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a 10-inch skillet. Spoon 1/4 cup of batter onto the hot skillet (like a pancake), making sure the vegetables are evenly distributed. Then sprinkle 1/4 of the shrimp on top.
5. Cook each side on medium heat for 2 minutes, until lightly browned. Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for another 5 minutes, occasionally turning and gently pressing the okonomiyaki with a spatula.
6. Prepare 3 more okonomiyakis as above. Keep the finished pancakes warm in a low oven while making the rest, or use 2 skillets and make 2 okonomiyakis at a time.
7. Serve hot with the sauce to taste (about 1 tbsp per okonomiyaki), and top with a sprinkling of nori.
YIELDS 4 6-INCH OKONO. -- SERVES 4.
